project_meta.name: Rural off-grid cottage — TT earthing (genuine TT)
project_meta.sprint_note: Authored in Sprint C.1 / M2 — the previously misnamed `intl-rural-tt/` folder was renamed to `intl-rural-tncs/` in Sprint A.1 because its content was always TN-C-S. This NEW example is the genuine TT case: rural off-grid dwelling, no PME, single rod electrode, RCD mandatory on every final circuit per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5.

ze_ohm_source: Off-grid installation — no utility supply, therefore Ze (network impedance) is effectively the local electrode resistance Ra. Installer-measured Ra = 200 Ω in dry season (worst case) on a single 3 m driven rod in sandy loam at ~800 Ω·m soil resistivity.

engineering_brief.site_summary: 80 m² single-storey off-grid rural cottage. No grid connection (no utility PEN bond → TT is the only valid system arrangement per IEC 60364-4-41 §411.5). PV-and-battery off-grid supply with inverter providing 230 V single-phase; inverter is configured with a single neutral-earth bond at the consumer side that establishes the system as TT (no utility-side bond exists).
engineering_brief.soil_summary: Sandy loam, ~800 Ω·m soil resistivity, dry season worst case. A single 3 m driven rod measured Ra = 200 Ω at handover. Engineer accepts this Ra value as the design value — calc.electrode_resistance tool deferred per WI3.
engineering_brief.rcd_strategy: Per IEC 60364-4-41:2017 §411.5 — TT mandates RCD on every final circuit. Disconnection condition §411.5.3: Ra × IΔn ≤ 50 V. With IΔn = 30 mA on every circuit and Ra = 200 Ω: 200 × 0.030 = 6.0 V ≤ 50 V (passes with margin). RCD type: §531.3.3 — Type A on socket-outlet circuits (≤6 mA DC residual tolerance, sufficient for modern domestic electronic loads); Type AC on the dedicated lighting circuit (no DC residual). F03 water heater is fed via socket-outlet spur in this design and takes Type A as precaution.
